Description: A solitary hunter and the largest cat species in the world, native to Asia.
Interesting Fact: Every tiger has a unique stripe pattern, much like a human fingerprint.

Description: The largest land mammal, known for its high intelligence and long trunk.
Interesting Fact: Elephants can communicate using low-frequency rumbles that travel long distances.

Description: The tallest living terrestrial animal, characterized by its extremely long neck.
Interesting Fact: A giraffe's tongue can be long enough to help it clean its own ears.

Description: A large marsupial native to Australia, known for hopping on its powerful hind legs.
Interesting Fact: Kangaroos use their strong tails like a fifth limb when moving slowly.

Description: A bear native to south-central China, famous for its black and white coat and bamboo diet.
Interesting Fact: Giant pandas spend much of their day eating bamboo to meet their energy needs.

Description: Flightless marine birds that live almost exclusively in the Southern Hemisphere.
Interesting Fact: Penguins are expert swimmers that use their wings as flippers underwater.

Description: A large canine native to Eurasia and North America, known for its pack behavior.
Interesting Fact: Wolves use howls to communicate with pack members across long distances.

Description: African equines best known for their distinctive black-and-white striped coats.
Interesting Fact: No two zebras have exactly the same stripe pattern.

Description: An arboreal herbivorous marsupial native to Australia, primarily eating eucalyptus.
Interesting Fact: Koalas sleep for much of the day because eucalyptus leaves provide limited energy.

Description: A large, powerful bird of prey with excellent eyesight and a hooked beak.
Interesting Fact: Eagles can spot prey from remarkable distances thanks to their sharp vision.

Description: Ancient predatory fish found in all oceans, known for their cartilaginous skeletons.
Interesting Fact: Some shark species have existed for hundreds of millions of years.

Description: Highly intelligent marine mammals known for their playful behavior and sonar.
Interesting Fact: Dolphins use echolocation to navigate and locate prey underwater.

Description: The largest living primates, inhabiting the forests of central sub-Saharan Africa.
Interesting Fact: Gorillas share a very high percentage of their DNA with humans.

Description: The fastest land animal, capable of reaching speeds up to 70 mph.
Interesting Fact: Cheetahs accelerate extremely quickly but can only sustain top speed briefly.

Description: A large cat species found in the Americas, known for its powerful bite.
Interesting Fact: Jaguars are strong swimmers and often hunt near rivers and wetlands.

Description: Primate native only to the island of Madagascar, with large reflective eyes.
Interesting Fact: Lemurs are found naturally only on Madagascar and nearby small islands.

Description: The largest and heaviest living bird, native to Africa and flightless.
Interesting Fact: An ostrich has powerful legs that can deliver a dangerous kick.

Description: A duck-billed, egg-laying mammal native to eastern Australia.
Interesting Fact: The platypus is one of the few mammals that lays eggs.

Description: A small arboreal mammal native to the eastern Himalayas and southwestern China.
Interesting Fact: Red pandas use their bushy tails for balance and warmth.

Description: A small fox native to the Arctic regions, known for its thick white winter fur.
Interesting Fact: Arctic foxes change coat color with the seasons for camouflage.

Description: A large bear species found across Eurasia and North America.
Interesting Fact: Brown bears can cover surprisingly long distances while foraging.

Description: A species of great ape native to the forests of tropical Africa, our closest relative.
Interesting Fact: Chimpanzees are known to use tools such as sticks to gather food.

Description: Social wading birds known for their pink feathers and standing on one leg.
Interesting Fact: Flamingos get much of their pink coloring from pigments in their diet.

Description: A large, mostly herbivorous, semiaquatic mammal native to sub-Saharan Africa.
Interesting Fact: Hippos spend much of the day in water to help keep cool.

Description: A genus of herbivorous lizards native to tropical areas of Mexico and South America.
Interesting Fact: Marine iguanas can forage in the ocean, while many other iguanas stay on land.

Description: The largest species of lizard, found in the Indonesian islands.
Interesting Fact: Komodo dragons can detect carrion and prey from impressive distances.

Description: Medium-sized wild cats known for their tufted ears and short tails.
Interesting Fact: Lynx are well adapted to snowy habitats with wide, furry paws.

Description: A small mongoose found in southern Africa, known for its upright sentry posture.
Interesting Fact: Meerkats take turns acting as lookouts while the group feeds.

Description: A medium-sized whale with a large 'tusk' which is actually a protruding tooth.
Interesting Fact: A narwhal's tusk is an elongated tooth that can grow several feet long.

Description: A giraffid mammal native to the Ituri Rainforest in central Africa.
Interesting Fact: Okapis are relatives of giraffes despite their zebra-like leg markings.

Description: A hypercarnivorous bear whose native range lies largely within the Arctic Circle.
Interesting Fact: Polar bears have black skin beneath their fur to help absorb heat.

Description: A small macropod about the size of a domestic cat, native to Western Australia.
Interesting Fact: Quokkas are known for a facial expression that often looks like a smile.

Description: Large herbivorous mammals known for their thick skin and prominent horns.
Interesting Fact: A rhino's horn is made primarily of keratin, the same protein as hair and nails.

Description: A large cat native to the mountain ranges of Central and South Asia.
Interesting Fact: Snow leopards use their long tails for balance and for warmth in cold climates.

Description: A carnivorous marsupial found in the wild only on the island state of Tasmania.
Interesting Fact: Tasmanian devils are named for their loud and fierce-sounding vocalizations.

Description: A playful semiaquatic mammal known for its thick fur and swimming ability.
Interesting Fact: Otters often use tools like rocks to crack open shellfish.

Description: Scavenging birds of prey that feed mostly on the carcasses of dead animals.
Interesting Fact: Vultures play an important ecological role by quickly cleaning up carrion.

Description: A large flippered marine mammal with a discontinuous distribution about the North Pole.
Interesting Fact: Walruses use their whiskers to help locate food on the seafloor.

Description: A species of small, translucent fish native to the Amazon River basin.
Interesting Fact: Its semi-transparent body makes the X-ray tetra easy to recognize.

Description: A long-haired domesticated bovid found throughout the Himalayan region.
Interesting Fact: Yaks are well suited to high altitudes with cold temperatures and thin air.

Description: A species or subspecies of domestic cattle originating in South Asia.
Interesting Fact: Zebu cattle are recognizable by the hump over their shoulders.

Description: New World mammals known for having a leathery armor shell.
Interesting Fact: Some armadillos can roll into a ball for protection.

Description: Large even-toed ungulates that historically roamed North American plains.
Interesting Fact: Bison can run much faster than many people expect despite their size.

Description: The largest living rodent in the world, native to South America.
Interesting Fact: Capybaras are highly social and often gather in groups near water.

Description: A wild dog found in Australia, likely descended from semi-domesticated dogs.
Interesting Fact: Dingoes are agile hunters that can adapt to a wide range of habitats.

Description: Spiny anteaters that are egg-laying mammals native to Oceania.
Interesting Fact: Echidnas are monotremes, meaning they are mammals that lay eggs.

Description: Birds of prey known for their incredible speed and keen hunting skills.
Interesting Fact: Peregrine falcons are famous for high-speed dives when hunting.

Description: A large subspecies of brown bear found in North America.
Interesting Fact: Grizzly bears can dig efficiently with their long front claws.

Description: A mammal known for its strength, ferocity, and tough skin.
Interesting Fact: Honey badgers are known for persistence and a broad, opportunistic diet.
